description: Mapping of SOX Section 404 IT controls to NIST 800-53 Rev 5

source.disclaimer: These mappings are interpretive guidance for IT compliance research. They are NOT official SEC or NIST crosswalks. For authoritative guidance, consult your auditors, legal counsel, and SOX compliance specialists. SOX Section 404 focuses on internal controls over financial reporting (ICFR), with IT general controls (ITGCs) as a critical component.
references: SOX Section 404 - Management Assessment of Internal Controls
NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - Security and Privacy Controls
source.methodology: Mappings focus on IT General Controls (ITGCs) that support SOX compliance: access controls, change management, computer operations, and data integrity.

mappings.rationale: SOX requires proper access controls to financial systems - account management is critical ITGC
Segregation of duties and least privilege for financial reporting systems
Prevent unauthorized changes to financial data and reports
SOX requires audit trails for all financial system activities
Audit logs must capture who, what, when for financial transactions

mappings.control_name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
mappings.rationale: Regular review of audit logs to detect anomalies in financial systems
SOX audit trails must be tamper-proof and retained
Change management is critical ITGC - all changes to financial systems must be controlled
Changes to financial systems require impact assessment
Financial data backup and recovery required for business continuity
Validate financial data inputs to prevent errors and fraud
SOX 802 criminalizes destruction of audit records - retention critical
SEC Rule 13a-15 requires risk-based approach to internal controls
